title: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
slides: Why is Big Tech suddenly begging for AI rules?
They once lobbied fiercely against any oversight.
Now they fear a smaller rival's open-source model.
Regulation could lock out new competitors entirely.
Watch which loopholes they carve for themselves this year.
thread_text:
1. Begging for rules?

Big Tech spent millions lobbying against AI oversight until a small rival’s open-source model surpassed their own.

2. The sudden switch.

They now claim regulation protects safety—but their real target is freezing out the newcomer who gave the code away.

3. Lock-out strategy.

Strict compliance costs hit small startups first. The giants already have legal teams ready.

4. Loophole carving.

Watch which exceptions they insert this year—self-certification, “research” exemptions, or grandfather clauses for existing models.

5. Your move.

Try an open-source model on your own device today. Do you trust the giants’ rules or your own test?
video_script: Big Tech begged for AI rules.
Now they fear a small rival.

script: Big Tech begged for AI rules.
Now they fear a small rival.

instagram.caption:
A tiny startup gave away an AI model that beat the tech giants’ best—for free. 🚨

The billion-dollar firms now beg for “safety” regulation. They spent millions fighting oversight until an open-source rival hurt their bottom line. Strict rules crush small teams first. The giants have armies of lawyers ready.

If you create with AI, your tools could lock up: paywalls, licensing fees, or a ban on the open model you used yesterday. 🔒

Did the startup who gave the code away, or the billionaires begging for rules, just earn your trust?

carousel_slides.description: Big Tech lobbied against AI oversight until an open-source rival surpassed their models.
Their real aim is freezing out the newcomer who gave the code away for free.
Strict compliance costs hit small teams hard; giants already have legal armies ready.
Self-certification and grandfather clauses could exempt their existing models from new rules.
Run a model on your own device and decide if you trust their rules or your own test.
